title: License Fee / Rent
text: The Licensee/Tenant shall pay to the Licensor/Landlord a monthly license fee/rent of INR {{occupancy_fee}} payable on or before the fifth (5th) day of each calendar month. Payment shall be made by bank transfer (NEFT/RTGS/UPI) or such other mode as agreed in writing. The Licensor/Landlord shall issue a written receipt for each payment. In the event of non-payment within fifteen (15) days of the due date, simple interest at fifteen percent (15%) per annum shall accrue on the overdue amount. The Parties agree that timely payment of the license fee/rent is of the essence of this Agreement.
statutory_reference: Transfer of Property Act, 1882 – S.105; Model Tenancy Act, 2021
name: License Fee / Rent

legal_basis.note: TDS on rent exceeding Rs.2.4 lakhs p.a.

legal_basis.act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017

jurisdiction_constraints.general: TDS under IT Act S.194I applies when annual rent exceeds Rs.2.4 lakhs — tenant must deduct at 10%

dispute_hotspots: TDS obligation under S.194I — commercial tenant must deduct TDS; failure creates IT liability
GST on commercial rent exceeding Rs.20 lakhs — landlord must charge 18% GST
Advance rent vs security deposit — different tax treatment
Cash payment restriction — Rs.2 lakhs cash payment limit under IT Act S.269ST
